commands.review_summary:
The PR updates inline usage dashboard mini bars to use provider branding colors and adds a focused bar-color test suite.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main's MiniUsageBars chooses its fill from valueStyle, and the inspected before screenshot shows provider-independent bar coloring. I did not run the app because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; maintainers should wait for CI and decide whether to merge the visual consistency change.

target.title: Fix/chart bars provider color
target.body: ## Summary Tints the inline overview usage bars with each provider's branding color instead of a fixed value-type palette. Previously `MiniUsageBars` chose its fill from `valueStyle` (golden for currency, purple for tokens, green for points), so every provider's bars looked identical and didn't match the provider's own switcher tab or detailed cost-history chart. Codex bars are now its teal, Claude its terracotta, and so on. ## Implementation - Add `barColor` to `InlineUsageDashboardModel`. - Resolve the color once in `inlineUsageDashboard(input:)` from `ProviderDescriptorRegistry…branding.color`, so every provider dashboard (cost-history, Poe, OpenRouter, Z.ai, MiniMax, DeepSeek, …) inherits it. - Have `MiniUsageBars` fill from `barColor`, keeping the height-based opacity ramp and the white hover state; the old value-type palette stays only as a fallback when no color is set. ## Verification - New `InlineUsageDashboardBarColorTests`: asserts the bar color matches branding for every `UsageProvider` (all 53 cases) and verifies the resolved dashboard carries the branding color end-to-end (Codex vs Claude differ). - Focused tests passed (new suite + `InlineCostHistoryDashboardLabelTests`). - `swift build` clean. - `./Scripts/lint.sh lint` passed: 0 violations, 0 serious across 1144 files. ## Screenshots Before: After:
target.author: elijahfriedman
